title: Marginal costing
summary: 9706 P2 — variable vs fixed costs, contribution, and marginal costing statements.

simpleExplanation.title: Costing for decisions
simpleExplanation.summary: Marginal costing treats fixed costs as period costs — only variable costs go into inventory; contribution covers fixed costs and profit.
steps: Fixed cost — period expense, not in unit cost.

flashcards.back: Sales revenue − Variable costs (per unit or total).
Whole amount charged to period — not in inventory value.
Absorption profit ± (Opening − Closing inventory) × fixed OAR per unit.
Absorption profit > marginal (fixed OH deferred in inventory).
Marginal costing better for short-term pricing/accept orders.
Direct materials + direct labour + variable production overhead.

sections.content: **Marginal costing** (2.2.3) — **contribution format** SPL is essential. Know **reconciliation** with absorption profit.

sections.question: Sales 2,000 units @ $40. Variable cost $22 per unit. Fixed costs $24,000. Prepare marginal costing profit statement.
Absorption profit $12,000. Opening inventory 100 units; closing 300 units. Fixed overhead absorption rate $6 per unit. Reconcile to marginal profit.
sections.solution:
**Inventory increase** = 300 − 100 = **200 units**
**Fixed overhead in inventory** = 200 × $6 = **$1,200**

**Marginal profit** = Absorption profit − increase in inventory fixed OH
= 12,000 − 1,200 = **$10,800**

*(Higher closing inventory → absorption profit higher than marginal.)*
